meta.title: GCP-PDE-5: Governed Pipelines, Warehouse Optimization, and Reliable Operations

domains.name: Preparing and Using Data for Analysis

questions.stem: A retail company needs to ingest clickstream events from thousands of applications and let multiple downstream systems consume them independently. Which Google Cloud service is the best fit?

options.explanation: Pub/Sub is the managed messaging service designed for decoupled event ingestion and fan-out. The other options are storage, relational database, or BI services rather than event buses.

questions.stem: A team wants a fully managed Apache Beam service for both batch and streaming pipelines without managing cluster nodes. Which service should they choose?

options.explanation: Dataflow runs Apache Beam pipelines as a managed service. Dataproc is for managed Spark/Hadoop clusters, Bigtable is storage, and Cloud Run jobs do not provide Beam pipeline execution.

questions.stem: A workload requires petabyte-scale SQL analytics over columnar storage with serverless query execution. Which service is the best match?

options.explanation: BigQuery is Google Cloud’s serverless enterprise data warehouse for large-scale SQL analytics. The other options target OLTP, document, or caching workloads.

questions.stem: A team stores time-series device readings and needs single-digit millisecond reads with very high write throughput. Which service is the best fit?

options.explanation: Bigtable is optimized for large-scale, low-latency key-value and wide-column workloads such as time-series data. BigQuery is analytical, Cloud Storage is object storage, and Spanner is relational with different access patterns.

questions.stem: An analyst repeatedly runs the same dashboard queries against a large BigQuery dataset and wants sub-second interactive acceleration. Which feature should be evaluated first?

options.explanation: BI Engine accelerates interactive BI queries for supported workloads in BigQuery. The other options address storage federation, governance, or messaging.

questions.stem: A data engineering team needs a managed Apache Airflow environment to orchestrate DAG-based pipelines on Google Cloud. Which service should they use?

options.explanation: Cloud Composer provides managed Apache Airflow. Cloud Scheduler only triggers jobs, Dataform focuses on SQL workflow development, and Data Catalog is metadata oriented.

questions.stem: A company wants to reduce BigQuery scan cost for a very large fact table that is commonly filtered by `event_date` and then by `customer_id`. Which design choices help most? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Partitioning reduces data scanned when queries filter on the partition key, and clustering improves pruning within partitions for common filter columns. Cloud SQL is not a substitute for warehouse-scale analytics, and disabling statistics would not improve pruning.

Partitioning reduces data scanned when queries filter on the partition key, and clustering improves pruning within partitions for common filter columns. Cloud SQL is not a substitute for warehouse-scale analytics, and disabling statistics would not improve pruning.

options.text: Store the data in Cloud SQL instead

questions.stem: A Dataflow streaming pipeline must tolerate late events while still producing timely windowed results. Which Beam concept directly controls how late data is handled?

options.explanation: In Beam and Dataflow, watermarks and allowed lateness control how out-of-order and late-arriving events are processed for windowed computations. The other options do not define event-time correctness.

questions.stem: A Bigtable table is experiencing hotspotting because new writes all target sequential row keys. What is the best redesign?

options.text: Use a row key with higher cardinality and better prefix distribution
options.explanation: Bigtable performance depends heavily on row-key design. Sequential keys concentrate traffic on a narrow range, so a better-distributed row key reduces hotspotting.

options.text: Increase column family count for every attribute
Convert the table to a BigQuery view

questions.stem: A team wants SQL-based transformation workflows in BigQuery with dependency management, testing, and version control. Which service best fits that requirement?

options.explanation: Dataform is designed for SQL workflow development on BigQuery, including dependency management and testing. The other options do not provide this SQL transformation workflow capability.

questions.stem: A production Pub/Sub subscription must keep failing messages from blocking delivery forever. Which configuration should be added? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Dead-letter topics and maximum delivery attempts route repeatedly failing messages away from the main subscription flow. Slot reservations and materialized view schedules are unrelated.
Dead-letter topics and maximum delivery attempts route repeatedly failing messages away from the main subscription flow. Slot reservations and materialized view schedules are unrelated.

questions.stem: A team runs existing Spark jobs with custom JARs, needs YARN/Spark semantics, and wants the least code change during migration to Google Cloud. Which service should they choose first?

options.explanation: Dataproc is managed Spark/Hadoop and is usually the lowest-friction path for existing Spark workloads. Dataflow would require Apache Beam patterns instead of native Spark semantics.

questions.stem: A compliance team wants object data to transition automatically from Standard to cheaper classes after 30 days. Which Google Cloud Storage capability should be used?

options.explanation: Lifecycle management applies rule-based object transitions and deletions across storage classes. The other options do not automate class transitions.

questions.stem: A company needs a cataloged, governed lake across projects and wants to manage data discovery, quality, and lineage centrally. Which service should anchor that design?

options.explanation: Dataplex provides governance and data management capabilities across lakes, zones, assets, and metadata. The other options solve unrelated infrastructure concerns.

questions.stem: An analyst wants to train and run a simple regression model directly in the warehouse by using SQL statements. Which feature should be used?

options.explanation: BigQuery ML enables model creation and prediction directly with SQL in BigQuery. The other options do not provide the same warehouse-native SQL experience.

questions.stem: A data team wants to monitor a production pipeline and trigger alerts when custom latency metrics exceed thresholds. Which Google Cloud service is designed for that?

options.explanation: Cloud Monitoring collects metrics and supports alerting policies for operational thresholds. The other options target build, CDN, or certificate management.

questions.stem: A batch pipeline needs to copy large on-premises file sets into Cloud Storage on a recurring schedule over the network with minimal custom code. Which service is the best fit?

options.explanation: Storage Transfer Service is built for managed data movement into Cloud Storage, including recurring transfers. The other services do not specialize in scheduled bulk transfer.

questions.stem: A BigQuery team has a dashboard query that is expensive but repeatedly computes the same pre-aggregated results over mostly unchanged source data. Which feature is most appropriate?

options.explanation: Materialized views persist precomputed query results and can improve performance for repeated aggregation workloads. The other options are unrelated.

questions.stem: A company must choose between BigQuery and Bigtable for a workload. Which requirements are stronger indicators for Bigtable? (Select TWO)

options.text: Single-row low-latency reads and writes at massive scale
options.explanation: Bigtable is a wide-column NoSQL service designed for high-throughput low-latency access patterns such as time series and key-value lookups. BigQuery is the better fit for ad hoc SQL analytics and joins.

Bigtable is a wide-column NoSQL service designed for high-throughput low-latency access patterns such as time series and key-value lookups. BigQuery is the better fit for ad hoc SQL analytics and joins.

options.text: Ad hoc ANSI SQL analytics over many tables

questions.stem: A Dataflow job must read from Pub/Sub and write to BigQuery while scaling workers automatically based on demand. Which statement is true?

options.text: Dataflow supports autoscaling for supported pipelines
options.explanation: Dataflow is managed and supports autoscaling for supported job types. The other statements are incorrect because Dataflow integrates with both Pub/Sub and BigQuery.

options.text: Autoscaling requires manually adding worker VMs
Pub/Sub subscriptions cannot be read by Dataflow
BigQuery can only be written by batch pipelines

questions.stem: A security team wants to grant a service account permission to read from one Pub/Sub subscription without granting broad project owner access. What should they use?

options.text: A least-privilege IAM role on the relevant resource
options.explanation: IAM should be used to grant the minimum permissions required to the service account at the appropriate scope. The other options do not control Pub/Sub authorization.

options.text: A firewall rule on the VPC

questions.stem: A BigQuery table is partitioned by date, but analysts often filter by date and `region` and still scan more data than expected. What is the best next optimization?

options.explanation: Clustering complements partitioning by organizing data within partitions around common filter columns such as region. The other choices would reduce performance or remove warehouse capabilities.

options.text: Move the table to Cloud SQL
Convert the table to CSV in Cloud Storage

questions.stem: Which statements about Cloud Storage classes are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: They trade off access cost and minimum storage duration for different access patterns
options.explanation: Storage classes are intended for different access patterns and costs, and lifecycle rules can move objects between classes automatically. They do not change object semantics into relational data stores.

options.text: They all provide the same low-latency access to objects
They determine whether an object is relational or nonrelational
Lifecycle rules can transition objects between classes
options.explanation: Storage classes are intended for different access patterns and costs, and lifecycle rules can move objects between classes automatically. They do not change object semantics into relational data stores.

questions.stem: A team needs to migrate an existing PostgreSQL database into Cloud SQL with managed replication and minimal downtime. Which service is specifically built for that task?

options.explanation: Database Migration Service is the managed service for migrating supported databases into Google Cloud targets with minimal downtime patterns.

questions.stem: A marketing team wants SaaS data such as Google Ads loaded on a schedule into BigQuery without building its own connector jobs. Which service is the best fit?

options.explanation: BigQuery Data Transfer Service provides managed scheduled ingestion from supported SaaS and Google sources into BigQuery.

questions.stem: A Dataproc cluster runs only overnight ETL and should reduce cost when idle. Which practice is best?

options.text: Use ephemeral clusters or autoscaling for the job workload
options.explanation: Ephemeral clusters and autoscaling are common Dataproc cost controls for intermittent workloads. The other options increase cost or reduce resiliency.

options.text: Pin the cluster size permanently to the maximum
Replicate the data to every region

questions.stem: A team stores regulated customer data in BigQuery and wants stronger governance before analysts can broadly access it. Which actions help? (Select TWO)

options.text: Classify and discover sensitive data with Sensitive Data Protection
options.explanation: Sensitive Data Protection helps detect sensitive content, and Dataplex supports governance, discovery, and management. Disabling audit logs and hard-coded credentials are poor security practices.

options.text: Use Dataplex governance and metadata controls
options.explanation: Sensitive Data Protection helps detect sensitive content, and Dataplex supports governance, discovery, and management. Disabling audit logs and hard-coded credentials are poor security practices.

options.text: Disable audit logs to reduce cost

questions.stem: Which BigQuery purchase model is most directly associated with dedicated query capacity measured in slots?

options.explanation: BigQuery compute capacity is associated with slots and reservation-based or edition-based capacity planning, not the other infrastructure services.

questions.stem: A platform team must design a streaming architecture where correctness depends on event time rather than processing time. Which technology pairing is most aligned to that requirement?

options.text: Pub/Sub with Dataflow windowing and watermarks
options.explanation: Pub/Sub provides ingestion and Dataflow/Beam provides event-time windowing, watermarks, and triggers needed for streaming correctness. The other pairings do not address event-time processing.

questions.stem: A data engineer wants to enrich streaming events with a slowly changing reference dataset and write results continuously. Which service is best suited for implementing that managed stream processing logic?

options.explanation: Dataflow is the managed service for implementing streaming transforms and enrichments at scale. The other options do not provide stream processing semantics.

questions.stem: Which service should a team use to centralize logs from Dataflow, Dataproc, and other Google Cloud services for troubleshooting?

options.explanation: Cloud Logging centralizes operational logs across Google Cloud services. The other services solve networking or security concerns rather than log management.

questions.stem: A BigQuery dataset contains highly queried summary tables that must stay fresh automatically as base tables change. Which feature reduces manual rebuild logic?

options.explanation: Materialized views maintain precomputed query results with automatic refresh behavior for supported patterns. The other options do not address warehouse summary maintenance.

questions.stem: A Bigtable application serves users from multiple regions and needs higher availability for reads if one cluster becomes unavailable. Which feature helps achieve that?

options.explanation: Bigtable replication across clusters improves resilience and can support multi-cluster access patterns. The other options do not provide serving availability for Bigtable.

questions.stem: Which requirements usually push a design toward BigQuery instead of Cloud SQL? (Select TWO)

options.text: Warehouse-scale analytical queries over very large datasets
options.explanation: BigQuery is designed for large-scale analytics and serverless warehouse workloads, whereas Cloud SQL is better for traditional OLTP applications and row-level transactions.

options.text: Serverless separation from traditional OLTP sizing concerns
options.explanation: BigQuery is designed for large-scale analytics and serverless warehouse workloads, whereas Cloud SQL is better for traditional OLTP applications and row-level transactions.

options.text: Tight transactional constraints for small relational workloads

questions.stem: A machine learning team deploys a model and wants alerts when prediction input distribution drifts significantly from training data. Which managed capability should they use?

options.explanation: Vertex AI Model Monitoring is designed to detect feature skew and drift for deployed models. The other services do not provide ML prediction monitoring.

questions.stem: Which statement about Pub/Sub pull subscriptions is correct?

options.text: Subscribers retrieve messages from the service when ready to process them
options.explanation: In a pull subscription, subscribers request messages when they are ready. The other statements are incorrect.

options.text: They require Cloud Storage buckets for buffering
They can only deliver one message per minute
They automatically convert messages into BigQuery tables

questions.stem: An analyst wants to keep detailed historical data cheap in Cloud Storage but query some of it from BigQuery without fully loading it into native storage. Which BigQuery capability supports that lake-style access pattern?

options.explanation: BigQuery supports querying external data and lake-style access patterns instead of requiring all data to be loaded into native tables for every use case.

questions.stem: Which design decisions improve BigQuery performance for common analytics workloads? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use partitioning where query predicates naturally align
options.explanation: Partitioning and clustering are core physical design optimizations in BigQuery. The other options reduce usability and performance.

options.text: Use clustering on commonly filtered columns
options.explanation: Partitioning and clustering are core physical design optimizations in BigQuery. The other options reduce usability and performance.

options.text: Store every field as a long JSON string
Replace SQL views with screenshots of results

questions.stem: A company needs a data platform that can manage metadata, organize data by business zones, and apply governance practices across lakes and warehouses. Which service best aligns with that responsibility?

options.explanation: Dataplex is built to organize and govern distributed data assets across zones and projects. The other services do not provide central data governance.

questions.stem: A production data platform team wants stronger operational reliability for pipelines. Which practices are most aligned with Google Cloud managed operations? (Select TWO)

options.text: Create alerting policies in Cloud Monitoring for SLA indicators
options.explanation: Monitoring alerts and centralized logs are foundational operational practices. The other choices reduce reliability or misuse managed services.

options.text: Review centralized service logs in Cloud Logging during incident response
options.explanation: Monitoring alerts and centralized logs are foundational operational practices. The other choices reduce reliability or misuse managed services.

options.text: Manually SSH into every managed worker on a schedule
Disable retries to surface failures faster
